title: Error: failed to read identity "C:\\Users\\redacted\\.ssh\\podman-machine-default": open C:\Users\redacted\.ssh\podman-machine-default: The system cannot find the file specified.
body:
### Issue Description

I installed Podman v4.4.1 through Podman Desktop. When I open PowerShell most `podman` commands don't work. 

```
PS C:\Users\redacted> podman version
Error: failed to read identity "C:\\Users\\redacted\\.ssh\\podman-machine-default": open C:\Users\redacted\.ssh\podman-machine-default: The system cannot find the file specified.
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman version


### Describe the results you received

```
PS C:\Users\redacted> podman version
Error: failed to read identity "C:\\Users\\redacted\\.ssh\\podman-machine-default": open C:\Users\redacted\.ssh\podman-machine-default: The system cannot find the file specified.
```

### Describe the results you expected

the podman version I have

### podman info output

```yaml
PS C:\Users\redacted> podman info
Error: failed to read identity "C:\\Users\\redacted\\.ssh\\podman-machine-default": open C:\Users\redacted\.ssh\podman-machine-default: The system cannot find the file specified.
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Windows 10

### Additional information


```
PS C:\Users\redacted> Get-Command podman

CommandType     Name                                               Version    Source
-----------     ----                                               -------    ------
Application     podman.exe                                         0.0.0.0    C:\Program Files\RedHat\Podman\podman.exe


```

Error I get through Podman Desktop when I hit "Initialize Podman":

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/103451171/229632583-9671a7bc-714b-475f-ad5e-23891f7dafe4.png)

_Re-copied for clarity:_
```
Error: Extracting compressed file
Importing operating system into WSL (this may take a few minutes on a new WSL install)...
A distribution with the supplied name already exists.

sh: line 1: /etc/containers/registries.conf.d/999-podman-machine.conf: No such file or directory
Error: the WSL import of guest OS failed: exit status 0xffffffff
```

comments.body:
> My issue got solved with https://podman-desktop.io/docs/troubleshooting#fixing-corrupted-podman-machine-in-windows

I'm not sure if the above played any role, but alone it did not help. Good to know that's there.

My issue was that after deleting my `podman-machine-default` machine to recreate it, Podman's default identity had changed to `C:\Users\<user>\.ssh\podman-machine-default2`, for whatever reason, and that file did not exist. It's possible I could've just renamed the pre-existing non-'2' files by adding a '2' to them, but I solved it via `podman`:

```shell
# Note the current values.
podman system connection list
# Rename '2' identities to non-'2's.
podman system connection rename podman-machine-default2 podman-machine-default
podman system connection rename podman-machine-default2-root podman-machine-default-root
# Overwrite the identity values with non-'2' values.
podman system connection add podman-machine-default ssh://user@localhost:3193/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock --identity "C:\Users\<user>\.ssh\podman-machine-default2" --default
podman system connection add podman-machine-default-root ssh://root@localhost:3193/run/podman/podman.sock --identity "C:\Users\<user>\.ssh\podman-machine-default"
```

In my case this did not immediately fix things; a `wsl --shutdown` and *re*-recreation of the Podman machine may be necessary (did the latter via Podman Desktop's _Settings_ > _Resources_). I also tried the application's troubleshooting options at this point too. Which of the three actions post the commands did it, if any, I'm not sure, but Podman's now back to working as expected.
